title: inula rhodia
intro: Inula rhodia, also known as the golden samosa or the Rosin root, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The roots of the Inula rhodia plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Inula rhodia include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and improving digestion. Inula rhodia can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to support the immune system and alleviate stress.
